GALANTAS GOLD ANNOUNCES THE EXERCISE OF WARRANTS
Toronto, Ontario - July 21, 2026 - Galantas Gold Corporation
(TSX-V: GAL | AIM: GAL) ("
Galantas
" or the "
Company
") announces the following exercises of warrants.
O
n 16 July 2026, warrants over 375,000 ordinary shares in the Company were exercised at an exercise price of
C$0.12
per ordinary share.
On 20 July 2026 warrants over 100,000 ordinary shares in the Company were exercised at an exercise price of C$0.12 per ordinary share.
Application will be made to the London Stock Exchange for the 475,000 new ordinary shares to be admitted to trading on AIM ("Admission"). It is expected that Admission will become effective and that dealings in the new ordinary shares on AIM will commence on or around 23 July 2026. The new ordinary shares will rank
pari passu
with the existing ordinary shares in the Company.
Following Admission, the Company's issued share capital will consist of 831,376,823 ordinary shares.
About Galantas Gold Corporation
Galantas Gold Corporation is a publicly traded gold and copper company focused on the acquisition, development, and advancement of gold and copper assets in stable mining jurisdictions. The Company is currently advancing the development of the Indiana Proj
ect and the Andacollo Gold Project in Chile. Galantas' strategy is to build long-term shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation, technically rigorous project evaluation, and responsible development of high-quality mineral assets.
Neither TSXV nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSXV) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this news release.

latest.title: Agreement to Acquire Crushing Plant for Andacollo
latest.content:
Today 11:51
RNS Number : 1173M
Galantas Gold Corporation
13 July 2026
GALANTAS GOLD TO ACQUIRE 20,000 TPD CRUSHING PLANT FOR ANDACOLLO GOLD PROJECT, CHILE
§
Major Milestone Significantly Advances Project Execution and Reduces Restart Capital
Toronto, Ontario - July 13, 2026 - Galantas Gold Corporation
("
Galantas
" or the "
Company
") (TSX-V: GAL | AIM: GAL |
OTCQX: GALKF
) announces
that it has entered into a binding agreement to acquire a complete three-stage crushing plant and associated agglomeration plant equipment located in Mexico for installation at its 100%-owned Andacollo Gold Project in Chile, for total consideration of US$4.2 million. Completion remains subject to execution of a definitive asset purchase agreement and satisfaction of customary conditions, including regulatory, corporate, export, import and other third-party approvals.
The crushing plant is designed for a nominal throughput capacity of approximately 20,000 tonnes per day and is reported by the seller to be in good operating condition, subject to Galantas' ongoing technical, legal and title due diligence. The acquisition represents a significant milestone in the Company's restart strategy and forms a key component of the planned development of the Andacollo Gold Project.
To execute the relocation of the plant, Galantas has appointed M3 Engineering & Technology Corporation ("M3"), an internationally recognized engineering firm with extensive experience in the design, construction and delivery of large mining projects throughout the Americas. M3 has commenced the detailed engineering and project execution activities, including plant documentation, disassembly planning, logistics, transportation engineering, and preparation for reassembly and commissioning in Chile.
With the crushing facility now secured, the Company expects gold production at Andacollo Gold to commence in Q1 2027.
Mario Stifano, CEO of Galantas, commented: "This acquisition is another major execution milestone for Galantas and materially advances the development of the Andacollo Gold Project. Securing a high-quality, proven crushing plant significantly reduces project execution risk, shortens the development schedule and represents substantial capital savings compared with constructing a new crushing facility. Together with our engineering partners at M3, we have now transitioned from project planning into active execution."
Robert Sedgemore, SVP of Operations of Galantas, continued: "Our project execution team is now fully mobilized at Andacollo Gold. Engineering has commenced, procurement activities are accelerating, site infrastructure work is progressing, and we continue to advance the permitting,
construction and operational readiness programs required to bring Andacollo Gold back into production."
Project Execution Underway
Galantas has assembled an experienced technical, engineering and operations team to lead execution of the restart program. Current activities include:
§
Detailed engineering for crushing plant relocation and installation
§
Site preparation and infrastructure upgrades
§
Procurement and contracting for key project work packages
§
Operational readiness planning
§
Mine planning and development activities
§
Recruitment and expansion of the Chilean operating team
§
Advancement of environmental, permitting and regulatory activities
Figure 1. Photos of the three-stage crushing plant being acquired by Galantas
About Galantas Gold Corporation
Galantas Gold Corporation is a publicly traded gold and copper company focused on the acquisition, development, and advancement of gold and copper assets in stable mining jurisdictions. The Company is currently advancing the development of the Indiana Proj
ect and the Andacollo Gold Project in Chile. Galantas' strategy is to build long-term shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation, technically rigorous project evaluation, and responsible development of high-quality mineral assets.
Neither TSXV nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSXV) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this news release. The Company is admitted to trading on AIM and accordingly, further disclosure on the matter can be found on the Company's profile on the London Stock Exchange website.
The information contained within this announcement is deemed to constitute inside information as stipulated under the retained EU law version of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 (the "UK MAR") which is part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018. The information is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of the UK MAR. Upon the publication of this announcement, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.

latest.title: Resignation of Chair
latest.content:
8 Jul 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4134L
Galantas Gold Corporation
08 July 2026
GALANTAS ANNOUNCES RESIGNATION OF CHAIR OF THE BOARD
Toronto, Ontario - July 8, 2026 - Galantas Gold Corporation
(TSX-V: GAL | AIM: GAL) ("
Galantas
" or the "
Company
") announces that Ms. Róisín Magee has resigned as a member of the Board of Directors and Chair of the Company, effective immediately. Róisín has decided to move to a Senior Role in private equity. The Board of Directors has nominated Mr. David Cather, a Director of the Company, as Interim Chair. Following the resignation, the Board of Directors of the Company consists of five directors.
Mario Stifano, President and CEO of Galantas, commented: "On behalf of the entire Board, I want to thank Róisín for her valued contributions to Galantas and wish her well in her future endeavours."
About Galantas Gold Corporation
Galantas Gold Corporation is a publicly traded gold and copper company focused on the acquisition, development, and advancement of gold and copper assets in stable mining jurisdictions. The Company is currently advancing the development of the Indiana Proj
ect and the Andacollo Gold Project in Chile. Galantas' strategy is to build long-term shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation, technically rigorous project evaluation, and responsible development of high-quality mineral assets.

company_30s.what_they_do: GAL.L is a small‑cap mining company that focuses on extracting and processing base metals, primarily copper and nickel, from the Andacollo project in Chile. The operation is still in the early development stage, with a crushing plant under construction and plans to bring the first ore to a smelter.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Base metals are essential for infrastructure, renewable energy, and electronics. Copper demand is set to rise as electric vehicles and smart grids expand, while nickel is critical for battery cathodes. GAL.L’s project sits in a region with proven mineral resources and a relatively low regulatory burden, giving it a potential competitive edge if it can bring production online.
company_30s.current_state: The company has recently raised capital through a private placement and is working on completing the crushing plant. It remains pre‑revenue, with a significant cash burn rate and a board that has recently seen a chair resignation. The shares are trading at a very low price, effectively a penny stock, and the market has largely ignored the company.

rns_narrative: The company is actively raising funds and building out its mining infrastructure, as shown by the private placement and the acquisition of a crushing plant for the Andacollo project.
Leadership changes – a chair resignation and a new CFO appointment – signal a shift in strategy and a push to streamline operations.
Warrants are being exercised and shares are being issued, indicating that the company is trying to shore up its balance sheet and generate working capital for the next phases of development.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: A quiet market that is largely indifferent, with occasional bursts of skepticism about the company’s ability to generate revenue.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No bullish chatter has surfaced yet; the silence itself is a contrarian sign.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the company’s heavy cash burn, lack of revenue, and the fact that it is still a penny stock make it a high‑risk play.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has stayed flat, with no real uptick in discussion. The few negative posts focus on the price gap between the current trading level and the original placement price.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead – no public search interest. This lack of noise gives the stock a big surprise window if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the crushing plant and the start of ore processing – the first production milestone.
Any regulatory approvals that allow the company to bring ore to the smelter and generate revenue.
A scheduled earnings release that shows a positive cash flow from operations or a significant reduction in debt.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that exceeds the 30‑day average, a price move above the 20‑day moving average, or a news item announcing the first production run or a partnership with a smelter.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early capitulation phase. The APEX compression score suggests the market is tightening, but the lack of a clear support level means the next big move could be triggered by a single catalyst. A mean‑reversion bounce is likely once the company demonstrates operational progress.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is trading at a fraction of its valuation potential. If the company can deliver its first production run, the price could move from the current penny level to anywhere in the 5‑10p range, representing a 400‑800% upside. The deep crash provides a large asymmetry: the downside is limited to the current price, while the upside is substantial if the project becomes operational.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the project fails to reach production, the company runs out of cash, or it faces regulatory setbacks. In that scenario the share price could fall to zero. The worst‑case downside is a total wipeout, but the probability is low given the recent capital raising and asset acquisition.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the current price (essentially nothing). Potential gain is several times the entry price if the project succeeds. The risk‑reward ratio is effectively infinite, making it an attractive contrarian bet.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce that breaks above the 0.005p level with at least 5‑fold volume over the 20‑day average. Set a stop just below the 0.003p support. Allocate 10% of your discretionary capital to this position and monitor for a 5‑day confirmation before scaling in.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear announcement of production or a partnership with a smelter. Also watch for a 20‑day moving average breakout or a volume spike that signals renewed investor interest.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, delisting, or fails to secure necessary permits, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: GAL.L sits at a deep crash with a silent market and no obvious catalysts; if a production milestone or regulatory approval materialises, the upside is huge relative to the negligible downside.
